methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
QuiX Quantum is a Series A quantum computing company that has raised €15M to date. The quantum computing sector comprises a sparse but growing set of comparable companies: three public peers (IonQ, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum) and eight private companies, including well-capitalized names such as IonQ, Quantinuum, Rigetti, D-Wave, and Infleqtion.

Conventional peer-median valuation proves inapplicable here. The quantum computing peer set lacks sufficient density at QuiX's stage and valuation tier—only one adjacent-stage comparable exists within the narrow filter, falling short of the three-minimum threshold required for statistical reliability. A broader sector-wide median would conflate early-stage risk profiles with later-stage or public-market multiples, introducing material distortion. The sector's nascent and heterogeneous nature (spanning hardware, software, and hybrid approaches) further undermines comparability.

Without triangulated methodologies yielding credible results, a defensible valuation range cannot be established from the data provided. To support a Series A valuation, additional methods—such as risk-adjusted DCF, structured expert elicitation, or buildout-cost analysis specific to quantum systems development—would be required alongside expansion of the comparable universe through later funding rounds or strategic transaction data. The current dataset does not permit quantitative synthesis.
